content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
May 11, 1990
Lance H. Ofson
olson, Connelly, Hage1, fonq & Leidigh
3oo capitol uaLl, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 9 58l-4
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-3 02
Dear Mr. Olson:
This is in response to your letter of APril 24, 1-990 request-
ing advice on behali of senator John Garamendi concerningr the
i"i"i".;= 
obliqations unaer tne PoLiticaL Reform Act (tha "Act")'1
OUESTION
May Senator Garanendi accept contributions from two general
partner-s of a business entity, lnade from their personal funds, if
iris carupaign has already received a 91,ooo contribution in the
forn of a iheck from the busi.ness entity which lras signed,
directed and controlled by a third qeneral partner?
CONCLUSION
Senator Garamendi may accePt contributions from two general
partners of a business entity even though the business entj'ty has
-aLready made a contribution of $1, ooo to his carnpaign ' 
Nei'ther
partner directed or controLled the contribution made by the
entity .
FACTS
Your office represents Senator John Garamendi. senator
Garamendi is a candldate for the Democratic nomination for State
Insurance Conmissioner.
1 cor"rrr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Section
18ooo, et Seg. A11 references to regTulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of ReguLatj-ons.
428 J Street, Suite 8O0 . P.O. Box 8O? o Sacramento CA 958o4-O8o7 o (916) 322.5660


Our Fi■e No. A-90-302
Page 2
According to the inforrnation that you provided, the senator's
controlled committee has received a $1,0oo contribution from a
business entity with three general partners. The contribution was
in the form of a check sigrned by one of the partners. 
You
i.ndicate that the contribution was directed and control,led by the
partner who signed the check. Each partner has the ability to in
tact direct and control political 
contributions made by the busi-
ness entity.
The other t$ro general partners did not participate in the
decision to direct and control the contribution from the business
entity. 
They would each like to make personal contributions to
the Senator's campaign in this same fiscal year.
ANALYSTS
The Act, as amended by Proposition 73, provides that-
contributions from persons to candidates for elective office and
to political 
cornmitlees must cornply with the f j.sca1 year contribu-
tr";-ii;ii;' 
i= t"t forth in secti6ns 85301, 85302 a;d 85303.3
The Act also sets forth contribution linits for special elections
and special runoff elections. 
(section 85305.) The purPose of
eropoiition 73's contribution lirnits was to pface a reasonabre
ceiiing on hosr much one donor can gj.ve to a candidate. (Argiument
in Favor of Proposition 73, California Ballot Pamphlet, June 7,
l-988 Prinary Election, p. 34, copy enclosed.)
Under some circuustances, the Act requires that contributions
nade by more than one person be cumulated and the persons be
treated as a single contributor to deterroine if the persons have
reached the contiibution linits of the Act. Cumulation is
required under two lines of authorj-ty- First, in 1976, the com-
miision set out standards for the cumulation of contributions in
two opinions, In re Kahn (1976) 2 FPPC ops. L51 and In re.Lumsdon
(1-g7 6i 2 FPPC ops. rao (copies enclosed). In the Kahn opinion the
iornmission concluded that tunulation of contributions was required
where contributions were made by a parent company and its wholly
owned subsj.diary. In the Lumsdon opinion, the commission found
cumuLation of c6ntributions was required where contributions were
made by a corporation and the corporation's rnajority shareholder'
2 Th" fiscal year is July 1 through June 30. (section 85102(a)')
3 contrj.butions from a person to a candidate are limited to
$1,ooo in any fiscal yeai. 
(Section 85301(a).) Contributions to
a political 
cornrnittee or broad based poli'tical committee are
linited to $2,500 per fiscal year per contributor. 
(Section
85302.) Contributions fron a political 
conroittee to a candidate
are lirnited to $2,500 each fiscal year, and contributions from a
broad based political 
conmittee or political party to a candidate
are limited to S5,Ooo per fiscal year. (Section 85303.)


Our File No. A-9 O-3 02
Page 3
Both opinions focused on the ability of one person to control the
contributions of another.
At the Cornmission's June 1989 meeting, Regulation 18531.5
(copy enclosed) was adopted to further clarify e/tren cumulation is
appropriate.a Specifically, where the question concerns two
contributors which are both entities, Regul,ation 18531.5 requires
cumulation if: 
(1) the sane person or a majority of the same
persons, (2) in fact directs and controls, (3) the decisions of
thro or more entities, 
(4) to nake contributions or expenditures to
support or oppose a candidate or candidates for elective office'
If there is no directi.on and control in fact by the same person or
rna j ority of persons as to either entity, cumulation is not
reguired. 
We have previously advj.sed that the term I'entitiestr is
not intended to include individuals. 
(Leidioh Advice Letter No.
I-89-637 , copy enclosed. )
In the situation that you describe, the guestion is the
cumulation of contributi.ons between an entity and an individual,
rather than between two entities. 
Therefore, we will not apply
Regulation 18531.5.
Analyzing the situation Pursuant to the Lumsdon opini,on, we
would say that a general. partner who has the authority to direct
and contiol the contributions of an entity nust cumulate his or
her personal contributions with those made by the business entity
at his or her direction and control . The assumption is that the
partner is acting to accomplish a common political 
goal. 
(see,
Leidiqh Advice Letter, supra. )
According to your letter, the two general partners who wish
to make personal contributions to Senator Garamendi "did not
participate in the decision to direct and control the contribu-
tionri from the business entity to the senator's canpaign.
Therefore, their contributions do not have to be aggregated with
the contribution from the busj,ness entity. 
However, the partner
who i,n fact dj.rected and controlled the business entity's
contribution must cumulate his contributions lrith that rnade by the
entity.
4 Regulation 18531.5 is in the rulenaking process. Althouqh not
currently effective, the regulation expresses the Commission's
policy concerning cunulation of contributions.


Our FiIe No. A-90-3 02
Page 4
I trust that this letter has provided you with the informa-
tion that you requested. If you have any additional questions,
please contact ne at (916) 322-59Of.
s incerely,
scott Hallabrin
